titleStmt.title: Letter from Robert Anderson to Edward O'Farrell, 9 May 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Robert Anderson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from Robert Anderson (b. 1860), the honourable secretary for the Irish Association of the Volunteers Training Corps, to Edward O'Farrell (1857-1926), the assistant under-secretary for Ireland. This was an association which comprised of men who were over the age to actively engage in military service set up to have a home defence force to prevent a German invasion. The letter notes that the full number of special constables required for the Dublin Metropolitan Police in the aftermath of the Easter Rising has been acquired. Anderson also notes this information has been sent to the commissioner, with whom he has only had verbal contact.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5611/8001/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Telephone-Dublin, 4772
IRISH ASSOCIATION OF VOLUNTEER TRAINING CORPS.
(Authorised by the Government as the recognised Central Body of all affiliated
Volunteer Training Corps in Ireland.)
18 South Frederick Street, Dublin 9th May 1916 My dear O Farrell, Thanks for your letter of the 6th inst., which
I have not had time to answer until now. We have secured the full number of Special
Constables required by the D.M.P. , and a letter to this effect has
been sent to the Commissioner with a request that their duty should
be clearly defined and that written application for their services
should be sent to us. So far, we have had nothing but verbal
communications with the Commissioner. Yours truly,
R.A. Anderson
Hon. Secretary
Sir Edward O Farrell, C. B., Assistant Under Secretary DUBLIN CASTLE
